chief_complaint: Two patients, ages 45 and 62, female and male, respectively, presented with symptoms of chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension.
history: Chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH) can lead to right ventricular (RV) ischemia and dysfunction due to chronic pulmonary artery obstruction and increased afterload.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG, bloods, and imaging studies, including cardiovascular MRI (CMR), were performed. CMR showed reduced biventricular perfusion before pulmonary thromboendarterectomy (PTE) and improvement in one patient after PTE.
